water.name: Arroyo Seco River (below waterfall)
aliases: Arroyo Seco River — lower main stem and tributaries below the waterfall

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(9) — Arroyo Seco River (Monterey Co.)
CDFW — Low Flow Fishing Regulations
sources.quotedText: Shall be closed to all fishing when the department determines that flows are impeding fish passage.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 238770, "Arroyo Seco", Stream, Monterey Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":238770,"gaz_name":"Arroyo Seco","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Monterey"} — geometry multipoint; one interior vertex (Web Mercator -13525436.2531, 4328248.0259) converts to lon -121.50106, lat 36.20052, ~0.9 km from the named first waterfall of the Arroyo Seco Gorge (36.208475, -121.499095 per RopeWiki's Arroyo Seco Gorge (Los Padres) route description, corroborating the same gorge reach roughly 3.5 river miles upstream of the Arroyo Seco Campground/Ranger Station). This waterfall — the feature that defines the boundary between the 7.40(b)(9) 'below waterfall' segment and the 7.50(b)(6) 'above waterfall' segment — is used as the representative point for this entry, replacing the file's previous coordinate (-121.4050, 36.2800), which was about 11.6 km away, well downstream of the actual waterfall/gorge area.

parameters.note: Open only on Saturdays, Sundays, Wednesdays, legal holidays, and the opening and closing days of the season; closed all other days within the Dec. 1 - Mar. 7 window. Applies to the main stem Arroyo Seco and tributaries below the waterfall located approximately 3.5 miles upstream from the U.S. Forest Service Ranger Station (the segment above the waterfall is separately regulated under §7.50(b)(6)). Also subject to Low-Flow Restrictions, Section 8.00(c).

regulations.humanSummary: Open December 1 through March 7, but only on Saturdays, Sundays, Wednesdays, legal holidays, and opening/closing days, on the main stem and tributaries below the waterfall roughly 3.5 miles upstream of the U.S. Forest Service Ranger Station
regulations.verbatimText: Dec. 1 through Mar. 7, but only on Sat., Sun., Wed., legal holidays and opening and closing days.

parameters.note: Only barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day, combined; 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead in possession, combined.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag limit; 4 in possession
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Also see Section 8.00(c) Low-Flow Restrictions: closed to all fishing when the department determines that flows are impeding fish passage.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to low-flow closure: closed to all fishing when flows are determined to be impeding fish passage

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
